title: Phebalium billardierei
intro: Phebalium billardierei, also known as Mountain Phebalium, is a small shrub native to southeastern Australia, characterized by its compact growth habit and clusters of small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in relieving respiratory issues and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Phebalium billardierei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phebalium billardierei is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical preparations for skin and wound care.
taxonomy: Phebalium billardierei is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to Australia and is also known as the silver-leaved phebalium. The classification of Phebalium billardierei is as follows: genus Phebalium, family Rutaceae, order Sapindales, class Magnoliopsida, and kingdom Plantae. This classification reflects its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Phebalium billardierei is an evergreen shrub with silver-grey foliage and produces white flowers.
